meta.title: AI training that gets your team using the tools you already pay for
meta.description: AI training, workshops and events for AI adoption. We redesign workflows so non-technical teams in Romania and the EU actually use Copilot, Gemini and ChatGPT.
meta.keywords: AI adoption, AI training, EU AI Act Article 4, Microsoft Copilot adoption, Gemini Enterprise rollout, AI for non-technical teams, workflow redesign, change management, AI literacy, Romania, Bucharest, EU, mid-market

pattern.title: Why AI adoption stalls after the rollout.
pattern.body: Most organisations respond to low AI tool usage with a second training session. Same result. The problem is not the tool and it is not the team. It is the process, which was never redesigned to use AI.

rows.text: Enthusiastic early adopters use the tool daily. Visible momentum.
Middle majority try it once or twice on familiar tasks. Irregular usage.
Usage plateaus. Most employees return to previous workflows. Dashboard shows "active licences." Processes have not changed.

premise.attribution: The premise behind every Unlearning School engagement
premise.quote: Adding a tool to an unchanged process does not change the process. You need to redesign the workflow first, and clear the old habit before the new one can form. That is the step most deployments skip.

items.situation: Copilot deployed. Licence spend is real. Process times have not improved. Board is asking for results.
items.result: One process rebuilt in one day. Measurable output. Template your team built themselves.

items.situation: You need a credible AI upskilling programme. EU AI Act Article 4 is in force. No plan that demonstrably changes behaviour.
items.result: Role-adapted workshops. Participants leave with 3–5 AI workflows ready the next morning.

items.situation: Tools are deployed and licensed. Adoption metrics are flat. Business teams blame IT for tools they never asked for.
items.result: A cross-functional adoption layer on top of your stack. Internal champions, governance, and EU AI Act audit trail.

items.title: AI for Non-Technical People
items.body: Structured adoption program for non-technical business teams. Modular: Discovery (1×2h), Short Sprint (2–3 sessions), or Full Program (4+ sessions). Built on the AI tools you already pay for.

items.title: Events & Keynotes
items.body: Workshops, hackathons, and keynotes for fast proof and internal momentum. 90 min – 2 days. 15 to 500 people. Every demo built around your workflows, not generic productivity examples.

items.title: Funding Structuring
items.body: Vendor MDF, AWS partner co-sell, or EU digital grants. We structure the program to qualify, draft the application, and align delivery so reimbursement actually happens.

mechanism.title: Unfreeze,
mechanism.body: Before a team can build new working habits, they must consciously let go of the old ones. Most AI adoption programmes start at "change" and skip "unfreeze" entirely. That is why behaviour does not stick. Our sprints are built around the unfreeze step first.
mechanism.footnote: Based on Lewin's Unfreeze–Change–Refreeze model (1947), applied to AI workflow adoption in non-technical teams.

faq.question: What does The Unlearning School do?
faq.answer: We help organisations turn deployed AI tools (Copilot, ChatGPT, Gemini) into actually-used workflows by redesigning processes and training non-technical teams. The focus is behaviour change, not tool demos.

faq.question: Who is this for?
faq.answer: COOs, HR/L&D leads, and CIOs in EU companies that already pay for AI licences but see flat adoption metrics and unchanged process times.

faq.question: Does this help with EU AI Act Article 4 compliance?
faq.answer: Yes. Our role-adapted programmes provide the AI literacy training required by EU AI Act Article 4, with auditable participation and behaviour-change outcomes.

faq.question: How do I start?
faq.answer: Book a free AI Adoption Gap Assessment. One working session, written report within 5 business days, no proposal attached.

scoreStrip.bodyEm: Get your score and a tailored adoption roadmap.

beforeAfter.body: One workflow, two timelines. Same team, same RFP, same outcome quality. The difference is where the work actually happens.

before.title: RFP review, the old way
steps: Highlight risks and obligations by hand
before.friction: Senior reviewer pulled off other work for a day

after.title: RFP review, with the same team
steps: Drop RFP into the team's existing Copilot
Run prompt sheet for risk + obligation extraction
Auto-fill internal template from structured output
Send clarifications in one consolidated email
after.friction: Reviewer keeps judgement, loses the typing

cases.body: We don't publish client logos. We do publish what changed: hours saved, processes rebuilt, people trained. Sectors and scale below are accurate; names withheld by agreement.

items.outcome: PMs trained on AI-assisted spec writing
Faculty and admin trained on responsible AI use
Reduction in weekly report production time

cases.footnote: Reference calls available under NDA after a first conversation.

founder.label: Founder
founder.lead: Your company already paid for AI licences. Most of them are barely touched. The problem is rarely the tool — it's that no one showed the team how AI fits their actual work.
founder.body: Before any session I research the company, the industry, and the departments in the room. Demos are built on workflows they already do. Sessions run 80% inside AI tools they already have access to. Everyone leaves with prompt sheets they can use the next morning, not a recording they'll never rewatch.
bullets: 20+ years across business leadership and education
Former General Manager, Bitdefender Romania & Moldova
Entrepreneurship Director, University of Life Sciences Timișoara

items.quote: Transformation begins with the commitment to intentional growth, and that is the meaning of this course. It exceeded expectations, and the one-on-one sessions with Răzvan had a major impact on my future artistic career.
items.name: Mary Calai

items.quote: The entrepreneurship course was extremely useful — I learned a lot from you, but I think it's only a small piece of what you have to offer. Wishing you continued success, and I hope more people get the chance to know you and learn from you.
items.name: Alina Nițu

items.quote: The course gave me the tools I needed to align personal development with professional growth and pushed me toward a holistic view of success. I confidently recommend The Unlearning School to any entrepreneur who wants to grow on both fronts.
items.name: Oana Chiva

items.quote: Răzvan separates fact from myth in personal development and entrepreneurship and delivers a complete educational concept. Well-structured, detailed, and the trainer's involvement was total.
items.name: Alex Tănase

items.quote: One of the most inspired and dedicated trainers I've met. Răzvan turns theory into practical experience and gives you not just solid business knowledge but the tools to apply it in real life — concise, yet all-encompassing.
items.name: Alexandra Zărnescu

items.quote: Thank you for the support and advice — it helped enormously. We wanted to let you know we placed 3rd. We did really well.
items.name: Andra & Erin
